title: Deliver globe-first surface with synchronized sphere and alternate views
goal: Make commonworld.net open directly on the interactive globe, keep the digital sphere attached to the visible globe through the side-layer transition, and add one settings gear that switches between globe and equivalent text presentation while exposing the existing catalog as a documented machine-readable surface.

approval.note: Use an isolated Commonworld worktree. Before merge require a complete downloadable GitHub diff, head/tree/diff-bound critical self-review, green CI, comment/review resolution, and exact live Pages readback.

acceptance.assertion: The root page is a viewport-filling globe surface from first paint; marketing copy, build status and the long catalog do not precede or visually compete with the globe.

The SVG digital sphere derives its rendered center and size from the visible MapLibre globe and remains attached during resize, camera motion and the side-layer transition; the displaced-ring defect is covered by a real geometry test.
Opening the digital sphere produces a coherent side-layer state of the same surface, with the globe retained as a subdued reference and exact close/back restoration including reduced-motion behavior.
Exactly one settings gear at the top right exposes presentation settings and switches between Globe and Text without hiding direct discovery controls or creating a separate product.
Selected CommonProject identity, active digital layer, search/filter state and browser history remain coherent across Globe and Text views.
The Text view presents the same catalog records and focus information without requiring WebGL and remains keyboard usable; no unsupported screen-reader product-support or WCAG claim is introduced.
The existing static catalog and project JSON files are documented and linked as the machine-readable surface, with stable manifest/schema/version metadata and no backend, write path or premature CLI.
Automated tests and visual/browser readback cover wide desktop, iPad-class and narrow mobile layouts before merge and the deployed Pages build is smoke-tested afterward.

metadata.source: operator screenshot of displaced digital sphere and direct globe-first instruction
